title: The Accusative Case (Akkusativ)
description: The real world doesn't just have doers; it has receivers. Learn how to say 'I see the man' properly.
explanation:
**HOOK**
Think of a basketball game. The Nominativ case is the player throwing the ball, and the Akkusativ case is the person catching it. We need a way to show who the catcher is!

**CONCEPT**
The Akkusativ is just your way of saying who RECEIVES the action. It is the Direct Object getting acted upon.

**RULE**
Here is the golden rule: In Akkusativ, ONLY the masculine article changes (it ends in -n). Every single other gender stays exactly the same!

tables.title: Akkusativ Changes (The Receiver)
tables.note: Notice the massive 'N' ending for masculine? der -> den, ein -> einen. Everything else is identical to Nominativ! Once you get this, the rest of German articles will start making sense.

deep_notes.title: WATCH OUT
deep_notes.text:
Most beginners try to change neutral and feminine words too! Remember, if it is not totally masculine, DO NOT touch it.
Also, common action verbs like 'haben' (have), 'brauchen' (need), and 'sehen' (see) always force the Akkusativ.

exercises.question: Welches der Geschlechter ändert sich im Akkusativ?
exercises.hint: Think about which gender turns into an N ending (der -> den).
exercises.explanation: Only Masculine words (der/ein) change to den/einen! Everything else is the same.

exercises.hint: Hund ist maskulin (der). Im Akkusativ braucht es ein -n am Ende.
exercises.explanation: Because 'der Hund' is the direct object (Akkusativ), it must change to 'den'!

exercises.question: Welcher Satz ist vollkommen richtig?
exercises.hint: Tisch ist maskulin (der), und du 'siehst' ihn.
exercises.explanation: Since Tisch is masculine, it must change to 'den'.

exercises.hint: Tasche is feminine. Does feminine change in Akkusativ?
exercises.explanation: Feminine words DO NOT change in Akkusativ! It remains 'eine'.

exercises.question: Ordne die Akkusativ-Artikel:
exercises.hint: Remember the golden rule: ONLY masculine changes!
exercises.explanation: Nail down that Masculine is the only one transforming to an 'n' ending!

exercises.question: Ordne den Satz:
exercises.hint: Subject first, then verb, then object.

exercises.question: ACHTUNG! Fülle die Lücke: 'Der Mann sucht ____ Kind'.
exercises.hint: Kind ist NEUTRAL (das Kind). Ändert sich das im Akkusativ?
exercises.explanation: Neuter (das) NEVER changes in Akkusativ! It stays 'das'.

exercises.hint: Autos is plural. Do plurals change in Akkusativ?
exercises.explanation: Plurals DO NOT change in Akkusativ! It remains 'keine'.

exercises.question: Schreibe 'ein Sohn' (der Sohn) im Akkusativ:
exercises.hint: Der maskuline Artikel 'ein' braucht eine '-en' Endung.
exercises.explanation: Since Sohn is masculine (der), 'ein' becomes 'einen' in the object position!

exercises.question: Ordne die Frage:
exercises.hint: Questions usually start with the verb!
exercises.explanation: Der Tisch is masculine -> den Tisch!
